2604.25887.title: No Pedestrian Left Behind: Real-Time Detection and Tracking of Vulnerable Road Users for Adaptive Traffic Signal Control
2604.25887.paper_summary: Current pedestrian crossing signals operate on fixed timing without adjustment to pedestrian behavior, which can leave vulnerable road users (VRUs) such as the elderly, disabled, or distracted pedestrians stranded when the light changes. We introduce No Pedestrian Left Behind (NPLB), a real-time adaptive traffic signal system that monitors VRUs in crosswalks and automatically extends signal timing when needed. We evaluated five state-of-the-art object detection models on the BGVP dataset, with YOLOv12 achieving the highest mean Average Precision at 50% (mAP@0.5) of 0.756. NPLB integrates our fine-tuned YOLOv12 with ByteTrack multi-object tracking and an adaptive controller that extends pedestrian phases when remaining time falls below a critical threshold. Through 10,000 Monte Carlo simulations, we demonstrate that NPLB improves VRU safety by 71.4%, reducing stranding rates from 9.10% to 2.60%, while requiring signal extensions in only 12.1% of crossing cycles.
2604.25887.paper_summary_zh: 目前的行人過路信號依賴固定的時間設定，並未根據行人的行為進行調整，這可能使得脆弱的道路使用者（VRUs），如老年人、殘疾人士或分心的行人，在燈號變換時被困。<lb/>我們介紹了「不讓行人掉隊」（NPLB），這是一個實時自適應交通信號系統，能夠監控人行道上的VRUs，並在需要時自動延長信號時間。<lb/>我們在BGVP數據集上評估了五種最先進的物體檢測模型，其中YOLOv12在50%（mAP@0.5）時達到了最高的平均精確度0.756。<lb/>NPLB將我們微調過的YOLOv12與ByteTrack多物體追蹤和一個自適應控制器整合，當剩餘時間低於臨界閾值時，該控制器會延長行人階段。<lb/>通過10,000次蒙地卡羅模擬，我們證明NPLB提高了71.4%的VRU安全性，將被困率從9.10%降低到2.60%，同時在僅12.1%的過路循環中需要延長信號。
2604.25887.author: Anas Gamal Aly et.al.
